classObjective: Describe how adding or multiplying every value in a data set affects the mean, median, mode, range, and standard deviation.
workedExamples: 9-6, Example 1 — Add a Constant to Every Data Value and Find Statistics
9-6, Example 2 — Multiply Every Data Value by a Constant and Find Statistics
warmUp: If everyone in a class gets 5 extra points on a test, what happens to the mean score?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: adding constants shifts mean, median, mode but doesn't change spread; multiplying changes both center and spread
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice predicting the effect of transformations on statistics
independentPractice: Practice problems analyzing how data transformations affect statistical measures
assessment: Exit ticket: if every value in a data set is multiplied by 3, what happens to the standard deviation?
capReflection: Why does multiplying by a constant change both the center and spread measures, while adding a constant only changes the center?
